name: RQ (Redis Queue)
what_it_does: Simple Python job queue backed by Redis with minimal configuration — enqueue functions and process them with workers.
use_cases: Quickly add async background processing to Flask/Django apps without Celery complexity
Offload slow API calls from request handlers to Redis-backed worker processes
Build simple fanout patterns by enqueuing multiple functions on different queues
Process agent-generated tasks with priority queues (high/default/low)
Implement delayed job execution with enqueue_in() for scheduled future work
not_for: Complex workflow DAGs with dependencies between tasks
High-throughput task processing where broker overhead matters
Non-Redis brokers — RQ only supports Redis as backend
best_when: Best for simple Python background job processing when Celery feels over-engineered and you already use Redis.
avoid_when: Avoid when you need complex retry strategies, workflow DAGs, or non-Redis broker support.

auth.notes: Library — auth via Redis connection URL (redis://:password@host:port/db).

pricing.notes: BSD licensed. Requires a Redis instance.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Failed jobs stored in failed queue with exception traceback accessible via job.exc_info.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No built-in idempotency — deduplication requires custom logic or job ID tracking.
known_agent_gotchas: Functions must be importable at worker startup — lambdas and closures cannot be enqueued
Job results stored in Redis expire after 500 seconds by default — increase result_ttl for long-lived result access
Burst mode workers (--burst) exit after queue is empty — not suitable for long-running daemon workers without restart loop
RQ does not support task chaining natively — use callbacks or separate queues for dependent task patterns
Failed jobs require manual requeueing via rq requeue or job.requeue() — no automatic retry without rq-retry extension

security_score_components.security_notes: Pickle serialization used for job arguments — only enqueue trusted data to prevent RCE via deserialization.
